era_context: Continuation of the Old Babylonian period in Mesopotamia; the Second Intermediate Period in Egypt with the 13th Dynasty weakening. The Indus Valley Civilization remained in its mature phase. The Hittite Old Kingdom had not yet formed; Anatolia was fragmented among smaller polities. This was a period of regional consolidation and long-distance trade, with no single dominant power spanning multiple regions.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Central Asia (limited archaeological evidence for this specific year)
disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. (No events documented for this year.)
historiographic_note: 1902 BCE falls within a period of sparse documentation. No specific events are recorded for this year in any known historical source. The year is archaeologically and textually invisible. Our understanding of this period comes from broader chronological frameworks (king lists, stratigraphic sequences) rather than year-by-year records. The absence of documentation reflects both the limitations of ancient record-keeping and the loss of records over time.
